name: English
description: The Australian Curriculum v9 English F-6 covers Language (understanding the English language system), Literature (engaging with literary texts), and Literacy (applying English knowledge across learning areas). Develops listening, speaking, reading, viewing and writing skills through diverse texts from Foundation to Year 6.
crossCurriculum: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Histories and Cultures
Asia and Australia's Engagement with Asia

Foundation.descriptor: Understand how texts are composed of words and groups of words that create meaning
Explore the effect of rhythm and rhyme in nursery rhymes and short poems
Know that spoken sounds and words can be written and read
Recognise and name all upper- and lower-case letters in sequence (alphabet)
Recognise the sounds (phonemes) associated with letters and know common letter-sound correspondences

Year 1.descriptor: Understand that texts are made up of words and groups of words and that words have predictable relationships
Recognise that each syllable has a vowel sound and use knowledge of syllables to decode unfamiliar words
Know most letter-sound correspondences for single letters, including consonants and short vowels
Recognise and name upper- and lower-case letters in sequence and relate each to the corresponding sound
Understand that a sound can be represented by multiple letter combinations and that some combinations can represent multiple sounds
Identify the roles and responsibilities of speakers and listeners in conversations
Understand the difference between simple sentences and compound sentences using 'and'
Recognise that capital letters are used for proper nouns and at the beginning of sentences
Know that nouns can be proper, common and pronouns, and that nouns are used to name people, places, things and ideas
Know that verbs represent actions, and that tense changes indicate time

Year 2.descriptor: Understand that word families are groups of words with common patterns of meaning and spelling
Recognise syllables and use knowledge of syllable count to decode unfamiliar words
Know most letter-sound correspondences, including long vowels and common digraphs
Understand that sounds can be represented by multiple letter combinations and that some patterns can represent multiple sounds
Understand how to use digraphs, long vowel spelling patterns and basic suffixes to decode unfamiliar words
Understand the structure of sentences and how sentences connect through the use of pronouns, conjunctions and reference
Understand the difference between simple and compound sentences using 'and', 'but', 'so'
Understand that nouns can be extended with adjectives and prepositional phrases
Understand the difference between statement, question, command and exclamation
Understand that texts can have different purposes and structures

Year 3.descriptor: Understand that words can represent people, places, things and ideas (nouns) and that they can also represent actions and qualities (verbs and adjectives)
Understand how morphemes and word families relate to each other
Know and use spelling patterns including prefixes and suffixes
Understand how to use phonic knowledge and morphological knowledge to decode unfamiliar words
Understand that words can be grouped by the type of relationship they have (synonyms, antonyms, homophones)
Understand the difference between main and subordinate clauses
Understand that sentence structures can vary for purpose and effect
Understand how quotation marks and dialogue are used in texts
Understand that different types of texts have recognisable structural features
Understand that paragraphs organise ideas in written texts
Understand that language can be used to describe people, places and things (adjectives) and that choices depend on audience and purpose

Year 4.descriptor: Understand that the meaning of words can change based on context and that this affects their use
Understand how knowledge of morphemes, word families and spelling patterns supports reading and spelling
Know and use spelling patterns including prefixes, suffixes and root words
Understand that vocabulary choices affect meaning and the overall impact of a text
Understand the difference between main and subordinate clauses and how they are used for emphasis and effect

Year 5.descriptor: Understand that vocabulary choices affect meaning and the overall impact of a text on the audience
Understand how knowledge of morphemes, word families, etymology and spelling patterns supports reading comprehension and spelling accuracy
Know and use a wide range of spelling patterns, including prefixes, suffixes and root words
Understand how to use a range of strategies to interpret unfamiliar vocabulary in context
Understand that complex sentences can include multiple clauses and that clause structure varies for purpose and effect
Understand how different types of sentences are used for different purposes
Understand the use of tense, aspect and voice in shaping meaning
Understand that paragraphs and other text features help to organise ideas for the reader

Year 6.descriptor: Understand how vocabulary choices affect meaning and how language is used to position the audience
Understand how knowledge of morphemes, word families, etymology and spelling patterns supports deep vocabulary development
Know and use a wide range of spelling patterns, including prefixes, suffixes, root words and etymological origins
Understand how to use a wide range of strategies to interpret and understand complex vocabulary in context
Understand how complex sentences can include multiple clauses and that clause structure varies for purpose and effect
Understand the use of tense, aspect, voice and modality to shape meaning
Understand how paragraphs function to organise ideas and how paragraphs relate to each other
Understand that authors use language to position readers and that this affects interpretation

Foundation.descriptor: Recognise and appreciate that texts can be read and heard in different ways
Identify features of literary texts, including characters and events, and use the name of text types (for example: story, rhyme, song)

Year 1.descriptor: Identify the purpose and intended audience of imaginative, informative and persuasive texts
Describe the characters and settings in imaginative texts and explain how characters respond to events
Recount key events in texts and identify the main idea
Recognise and discuss the effect of imaginative language, including rhyme, rhythm and patterns
Identify structural features of familiar text types, including stories, informative texts and poems

Year 2.descriptor: Identify the purpose and intended audience of imaginative, informative and persuasive texts
Describe the characters, settings and events in imaginative texts, and explain how characters respond to events
Identify and describe the main idea and supporting ideas in informative texts
Recognise that imaginative texts use language to create images and express ideas
Discuss the features of different text types, including stories, reports and poetry

Year 3.descriptor: Identify the purpose and intended audience of imaginative, informative and persuasive texts
Describe the characters, settings and events in imaginative texts and explain how characters respond to events
Identify the main idea and supporting ideas in informative texts
Recognise that imaginative texts use language to create images and express ideas
Discuss the features of different text types, including stories, reports and poetry

Year 4.descriptor: Identify the purpose and intended audience of imaginative, informative and persuasive texts
Describe the characters, settings and events in imaginative texts and explain how characters respond to events
Identify the main idea and supporting ideas in informative texts
Recognise that imaginative texts use language to create images and express ideas
Discuss the features of different text types, including stories, reports and poetry

Year 5.descriptor: Identify the purpose and intended audience of imaginative, informative and persuasive texts
Describe how characters, settings and events in imaginative texts are developed and how characters respond to events
Identify the main idea and supporting ideas in informative texts and evaluate the reliability of sources
Recognise how imaginative texts use language to create images, express ideas and create mood
Analyse the structural and language features of different text types

Year 6.descriptor: Identify the purpose and intended audience of imaginative, informative and persuasive texts
Describe how characters, settings and events in imaginative texts are developed and how characters respond to events
Identify the main idea and supporting ideas in informative texts and evaluate the reliability and relevance of sources
Recognise how imaginative texts use language to create images, express ideas, create mood and position readers
Analyse the structural and language features of different text types and explain how they shape meaning

Foundation.descriptor: Identify some differences between imaginative and informative texts
Listen to and respond to texts, drawing on personal experience and making connections with familiar objects and events
Create and present short spoken and written texts, using familiar words and simple sentences
Read simple sentences with emerging accuracy, fluency and understanding
Use learned sound-letter correspondences to spell words with short vowels and common consonants
Write upper- and lower-case letters using correct letter formation
Construct simple sentences using correct case and punctuation
Participate in reading and writing activities, including shared reading and guided writing, with support
Use vocabulary with emerging confidence, including high-frequency words and topic-specific terms

Year 1.descriptor: Read and understand short texts with simple sentences and some unfamiliar vocabulary
Use phonological and semantic information to read and understand texts, including sounding out unfamiliar words
Read texts with increasing fluency, accuracy and expression
Read aloud with developing fluency and confidence
Use spelling knowledge to attempt the spelling of unfamiliar words
Write short texts with appropriate word choice, simple sentences and correct punctuation
Use grammatical features including correct tense, subject-verb agreement and simple conjunction
Create short written and multimodal texts for a specific purpose and audience
Listen and respond to texts, making connections with personal experience
Participate in spoken situations, including conversations and group discussions
Discuss ideas and experiences using vocabulary from reading and other learning experiences
Identify how texts make connections between ideas and people
Plan and organise short spoken and written texts for particular purposes
Re-read and edit texts for meaning, appropriate vocabulary and sentence structure
Use speaking and listening to share ideas and information in structured situations

Year 2.descriptor: Read and understand texts with varied sentence structures and increasing vocabulary
Use knowledge of letter-sound correspondences, digraphs and spelling patterns to decode
Read texts with developing fluency, accuracy and confidence
Use semantic and syntactic information to confirm attempts to decode
Use spelling knowledge to spell familiar and unfamiliar words
Write texts with increasing complexity, appropriate structure and accurate grammar
Create written and multimodal texts for different purposes and audiences
Use punctuation to clarify meaning, including periods, question marks and exclamation marks
Participate in spoken interactions, including conversations and group discussions
Analyse ideas and information in informative texts
Discuss characters and events in literary texts and make connections
Plan, draft and publish written and multimodal texts with appropriate structure and accurate grammar

Year 3.descriptor: Read and understand texts with varied sentence structures, varied vocabulary and some unfamiliar content
Use knowledge of letter-sound correspondences, morphemes and context to decode
Read texts with developing fluency, accuracy and independence
Use spelling knowledge to spell familiar and unfamiliar words accurately
Write texts for different purposes with accurate grammar, coherent structure and appropriate punctuation
Use paragraphs to organise written ideas
Create written and multimodal texts for different purposes and audiences
Participate in spoken interactions, making contributions and building on others' ideas
Analyse ideas and information in informative texts
Respond to literature, discussing themes, characters and ideas
Plan, draft and publish written and multimodal texts with accurate grammar and coherent structure

Year 4.descriptor: Read and understand texts with varied sentence structures, varied vocabulary and increasingly unfamiliar content
Use knowledge of letter-sound correspondences, morphemes and context to decode complex words
Read texts with developing fluency, accuracy and independence
Use spelling knowledge to spell familiar and unfamiliar words accurately
Write texts for different purposes with accurate grammar, coherent structure and appropriate punctuation
Use paragraphs to organise written ideas for different purposes
Create written and multimodal texts for different purposes and audiences
Participate in spoken interactions, making contributions and building on others' ideas
Analyse ideas and information in informative texts
Respond to literature, discussing themes, characters and ideas
Plan, draft and publish written and multimodal texts with accurate grammar and coherent structure
Understand how to use reference materials, including dictionaries and thesauruses

Year 5.descriptor: Read and understand a range of texts with varied sentence structures, varied vocabulary and increasingly unfamiliar content
Use knowledge of morphemes, word families, etymology and context to interpret and understand unfamiliar vocabulary
Read texts with developing fluency, accuracy and independence across a range of text types
Use spelling knowledge to spell familiar and unfamiliar words accurately, including words with complex patterns
Write texts for different purposes with accurate grammar, coherent structure and appropriate punctuation
Use paragraphs to organise written ideas for different purposes
Create written and multimodal texts for different purposes and audiences
Participate in spoken interactions, making contributions and building on others' ideas
Analyse ideas and information in informative texts, identifying point of view and bias
Respond to and interpret literature, discussing themes, characterisation and ideas
Plan, draft and publish written and multimodal texts with accurate grammar, coherent structure and appropriate punctuation
Use a range of reference materials, including dictionaries, thesauruses and digital resources

Year 6.descriptor: Read and understand a range of texts with varied sentence structures, varied vocabulary and complex content
Use knowledge of morphemes, word families, etymology, context and literary references to interpret and understand complex vocabulary
Read texts with developing fluency, accuracy and independence across a range of text types and complexity
Use spelling knowledge to spell familiar and unfamiliar words accurately, including words with complex patterns and etymological origins
Write texts for different purposes with accurate grammar, coherent structure and appropriate punctuation
Use paragraphs and complex sentences to organise written ideas for different purposes
Create written and multimodal texts for different purposes and audiences
Participate in spoken interactions, making contributions and building on others' ideas
Analyse ideas and information in informative texts, identifying point of view, bias and intended audience
Respond to and interpret literature, discussing themes, characterisation, ideas and language choices
Plan, draft and publish written and multimodal texts with accurate grammar, coherent structure and appropriate punctuation
Use a wide range of reference materials, including dictionaries, thesauruses, digital resources and web-based tools

name: Mathematics
description: The Australian Curriculum v9 Mathematics F-6 covers Number, Algebra, Measurement, Space, Statistics and Probability. Develops fluency, reasoning and problem-solving through the six strands from Foundation to Year 6, integrating mathematical thinking with real-world contexts.
crossCurriculum: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Histories and Cultures
Asia and Australia's Engagement with Asia

Foundation.descriptor: Name, represent and order numbers to at least 20
Subitise small collections (instantly judge quantity without counting)
Compare the size of collections to at least 20
Partition and combine collections up to 20
Represent practical situations that involve addition and subtraction

Year 1.descriptor: Count to at least 100
Read and write numerals to at least 100
Recognise patterns to 20
Compare and order collections to at least 50
Partition collections to at least 20
Use a range of strategies to add and subtract within 20
Recognise Australian coins

Year 2.descriptor: Count to at least 1000
Read and write three-digit numbers
Use place value to partition three-digit numbers
Compare and order three-digit numbers
Use a range of strategies to add and subtract within 100
Recall multiplication facts for 2, 5 and 10
Recognise and explain the connection between multiplication and division
Use money — recognise combinations to $2

Year 3.descriptor: Recognise, represent and order numbers to at least 10 000
Partition, rename and represent three-digit numbers
Use efficient mental and written strategies for addition and subtraction
Recall multiplication facts for 2, 3, 5 and 10
Recall division facts for 2, 3, 5 and 10
Represent money values in various ways
Calculate change from transactions

Year 4.descriptor: Recognise, represent and order numbers to at least 10 000
Use place value to partition and rename numbers
Use efficient mental and written strategies for all four operations
Recall multiplication facts to 10 x 10
Use estimation to check calculations
Represent money values in various ways
Recognise decimal notation for tenths and hundredths

Year 5.descriptor: Use place value to at least 1 000 000
Use equivalent fractions, decimals and percentages
Solve problems using all four operations with whole numbers and decimals
Use efficient mental and written strategies for all four operations
Check the reasonableness of calculations

Year 6.descriptor: Use place value for large numbers and decimals
Use equivalent fractions, decimals and percentages
Solve problems involving all four operations with fractions, decimals and percentages
Use ratios in practical situations
Use efficient mental and written strategies for all four operations

Foundation.descriptor: Recognise, copy and continue repeating patterns
Sort and classify objects into groups

Year 1.descriptor: Recognise, continue and create pattern sequences
Solve practical problems involving addition and subtraction

Year 2.descriptor: Solve practical addition and subtraction problems
Continue and create patterns with addition and subtraction
Describe patterns in number sequences

Year 3.descriptor: Add and subtract multiples of 10 and 100
Solve problems using number sentences
Identify missing numbers in addition and subtraction equations

Year 4.descriptor: Find unknown values in number sentences
Continue and create number patterns
Use equivalence in number sentences

Year 5.descriptor: Use equivalence to continue and create number patterns
Find unknown values in equations
Solve word problems using number sentences

Year 6.descriptor: Continue and create number patterns
Solve equations using algebra
Use ordered pairs to describe position

Foundation.descriptor: Identify and compare the mass of objects
Identify the duration of events
Compare length, capacity and mass

Year 1.descriptor: Measure length using informal units
Describe duration using direct and indirect comparisons
Tell time to the half hour
Compare mass using balance scales

Year 2.descriptor: Measure length using informal units
Measure mass using balance scales
Tell time to the quarter hour
Compare and order duration of events

Year 3.descriptor: Measure in metres, centimetres and millimetres
Measure mass in kilograms and grams
Measure capacity in litres and millilitres
Tell time to the minute
Solve problems involving duration

Year 4.descriptor: Use scaled instruments for measurement
Use appropriate units for length, mass and capacity
Convert between units of length, mass and capacity
Calculate perimeter and area of rectangles
Solve problems involving time duration

Year 5.descriptor: Choose and use appropriate metric units for length, area, mass and capacity
Calculate perimeter and area of rectangles
Solve problems involving duration
Connect volume and capacity

Year 6.descriptor: Connect decimal representations to the metric system
Solve problems involving length, mass, capacity and volume
Solve problems involving perimeter, area and volume
Calculate angles on a straight line and at a point

Foundation.descriptor: Name and sort 2D shapes
Locate positions in space
Describe the location of an object from two perspectives

Year 1.descriptor: Sort and name 2D shapes
Locate positions in space
Recognise 3D objects in the environment

Year 2.descriptor: Describe and draw 2D shapes
Describe line symmetry
Describe features of 3D objects

Year 3.descriptor: Identify right angles, acute angles and obtuse angles
Compare angles to a right angle
Describe and compare 2D shapes by their properties
Locate and describe points on a grid

Year 4.descriptor: Explain and compare geometric properties
Apply enlargement transformation
Interpret grid reference systems

Year 5.descriptor: Estimate and measure angles in degrees
Use coordinates to locate positions
Describe translations, reflections and rotations
Apply geometric properties to solve problems

Year 6.descriptor: Classify triangles and quadrilaterals
Apply geometric properties to solve problems
Describe results of translations, reflections and rotations

Foundation.descriptor: Collect and sort data into categories

Year 1.descriptor: Collect and record data

Year 2.descriptor: Collect, classify and represent data
Interpret data displays

Year 3.descriptor: Collect, compare and interpret data
Describe data using comparative language

Year 4.descriptor: Collect and interpret data
Construct data displays

Year 5.descriptor: Collect, interpret and represent data
Interpret and compare data displays

Year 6.descriptor: Interpret and compare data displays
Calculate mean, median and range
Pose questions and collect data

Foundation.descriptor: Describe the likelihood of everyday events

Year 1.descriptor: Describe the outcome of familiar events as 'likely' or 'unlikely'

Year 2.descriptor: Describe the likelihood of outcomes in everyday events

Year 3.descriptor: Conduct chance experiments
Describe possible outcomes and likelihood

Year 4.descriptor: Describe probabilities using fractions, decimals and percentages
Conduct probability experiments

Year 5.descriptor: List outcomes of chance experiments
Recognise that probabilities range from 0 to 1

Year 6.descriptor: Describe probabilities using fractions, decimals and percentages
Conduct probability experiments
Compare observed frequencies with expected frequencies

name: Science
description: The Australian Curriculum v9 Science F-6 explores Biological, Chemical, Earth and Space, and Physical sciences. Students develop inquiry skills and understanding of the natural world through hands-on investigation, building scientific literacy from Foundation to Year 6.
crossCurriculum: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Histories and Cultures

name: HASS
description: The Australian Curriculum v9 HASS F-6 covers History, Geography, Civics and Citizenship, and Economics and Business. Develops understanding of society, environment and civic participation through inquiry-based learning.
crossCurriculum: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Histories and Cultures

name: Technologies
description: The Australian Curriculum v9 Technologies F-6 covers Digital Technologies and Design & Technologies. Students learn to create digital solutions and design technologies for real-world problems through project-based learning.

name: The Arts
description: The Australian Curriculum v9 The Arts F-6 covers Visual Arts, Music, Drama and Dance. Students develop creativity, expression and artistic skills across the four arts disciplines through making, performing and responding.
crossCurriculum: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Histories and Cultures

name: Health & Physical Education
description: The Australian Curriculum v9 HPE F-6 covers Health and Physical Education. Students develop knowledge, skills and attitudes for healthy, active lives and positive wellbeing through movement, health learning and safety.

name: Languages
description: The Australian Curriculum v9 Languages F-6 covers Italian, Japanese, Mandarin and Indonesian. Students develop communicative competence and intercultural understanding in the target language through listening, speaking, reading and writing.
crossCurriculum: Asia and Australia's Engagement with Asia
